stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 39% and 53%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $168K in good patient genotype, $24K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $250K in good patient genotype, $27K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $219K in good patient genotype, $71K in bad patient genotype. combination therapy: $250K in good patient genotype, $82K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.46: best option is combination therapy with EV=$159.3K. combination therapy is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [147.5, 171.0].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
combination therapy is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
